content.problemStatement:
You are given a small graph GsG_sGs​ with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges.

Let's create a big graph GbG_bGb​ with NKNKNK nodes (numbered 111 through NKNKNK) and MKMKMK edges. GbG_bGb​ consists of KKK copies of GsG_sGs​ ― formally, for each kkk (0≤k≤K−10 \le k \le K-10≤k≤K−1) and each edge between nodes uuu and vvv in GsG_sGs​, there is an edge between nodes u+kNu+kNu+kN and v+kNv+kNv+kN in GbG_bGb​.

Afterwards, let's take the complement of GbG_bGb​ and call it HHH ― formally, HHH is a graph with NKNKNK nodes (numbered 111 through NKNKNK) such that for each pair of nodes, there is an edge between these two nodes in HHH if and only if there is no edge between these two nodes in GbG_bGb​.

Count the number of spanning trees in HHH. Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: GsG_sGs​ does not contain any self-loops or multi-edges
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb denoting that the nodes aaa and bbb in GsG_sGs​ are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of spanning trees in HHH modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: HHH is the complete graph on three nodes, so the number of spanning trees is 333 (any two of the edges form a spanning tree).
